speaker: KING
text: Now must your conscience my acquittance seal,

speaker: KING
text: And you must put me in your heart for friend,

speaker: KING
text: Sith you have heard, and with a knowing ear,

speaker: KING
text: That he which hath your noble father slain

speaker: KING
text: Pursued my life.

speaker: LAERTES
text: It well appears. But tell me

speaker: LAERTES
text: Why you proceeded not against these feats,

speaker: LAERTES
text: So criminal and so capital in nature,

speaker: LAERTES
text: As by your safety, greatness, wisdom, all things else,

speaker: LAERTES
text: You mainly were stirred up.

speaker: KING
text: O, for two special reasons,

speaker: KING
text: Which may to you perhaps seem much unsinewed,

speaker: KING
text: But yet to me they’re strong. The Queen his mother

speaker: KING
text: Lives almost by his looks, and for myself

speaker: KING
text: (My virtue or my plague, be it either which),

speaker: KING
text: She is so conjunctive to my life and soul

speaker: KING
text: That, as the star moves not but in his sphere,

speaker: KING
text: I could not but by her. The other motive

speaker: KING
text: Why to a public count I might not go

speaker: KING
text: Is the great love the general gender bear him,

speaker: KING
text: Who, dipping all his faults in their affection,

speaker: KING
text: Work like the spring that turneth wood to stone,

speaker: KING
text: Convert his gyves to graces, so that my arrows,

speaker: KING
text: Too slightly timbered for so loud a wind,

speaker: KING
text: Would have reverted to my bow again,

speaker: KING
text: But not where I have aimed them.

speaker: LAERTES
text: And so have I a noble father lost,

speaker: LAERTES
text: A sister driven into desp’rate terms,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Whose worth, if praises may go back again,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Stood challenger on mount of all the age

speaker: LAERTES
text: For her perfections. But my revenge will come.

speaker: KING
text: Break not your sleeps for that. You must not think

speaker: KING
text: That we are made of stuff so flat and dull

speaker: KING
text: That we can let our beard be shook with danger

speaker: KING
text: And think it pastime. You shortly shall hear more.

speaker: KING
text: I loved your father, and we love ourself,

speaker: KING
text: And that, I hope, will teach you to imagine—

speaker: KING
text: How now? What news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Letters, my lord, from Hamlet.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: These to your Majesty, this to the Queen.

speaker: KING
text: From Hamlet? Who brought them?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Sailors, my lord, they say. I saw them not.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They were given me by Claudio. He received them

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of him that brought them.

speaker: KING
text: Laertes, you shall hear them.—

speaker: KING
text: Leave us.

speaker: KING
text: What should this mean? Are all the rest come back?

speaker: KING
text: Or is it some abuse and no such thing?

speaker: LAERTES
text: Know you the hand?

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis Hamlet’s character. “Naked”—

speaker: KING
text: And in a postscript here, he says “alone.”

speaker: KING
text: Can you advise me?

speaker: LAERTES
text: I am lost in it, my lord. But let him come.

speaker: LAERTES
text: It warms the very sickness in my heart

speaker: LAERTES
text: That I shall live and tell him to his teeth

speaker: LAERTES
text: “Thus didst thou.”

speaker: KING
text: If it be so, Laertes

speaker: KING
text: (As how should it be so? how otherwise?),

speaker: KING
text: Will you be ruled by me?

speaker: LAERTES
text: Ay, my lord,

speaker: LAERTES
text: So you will not o’errule me to a peace.

speaker: KING
text: To thine own peace. If he be now returned,

speaker: KING
text: As checking at his voyage, and that he means

speaker: KING
text: No more to undertake it, I will work him

speaker: KING
text: To an exploit, now ripe in my device,

speaker: KING
text: Under the which he shall not choose but fall;

speaker: KING
text: And for his death no wind of blame shall breathe,

speaker: KING
text: But even his mother shall uncharge the practice

speaker: KING
text: And call it accident.

speaker: LAERTES
text: My lord, I will be ruled,

speaker: LAERTES
text: The rather if you could devise it so

speaker: LAERTES
text: That I might be the organ.

speaker: KING
text: It falls right.

speaker: KING
text: You have been talked of since your travel much,

speaker: KING
text: And that in Hamlet’s hearing, for a quality

speaker: KING
text: Wherein they say you shine. Your sum of parts

speaker: KING
text: Did not together pluck such envy from him

speaker: KING
text: As did that one, and that, in my regard,

speaker: KING
text: Of the unworthiest siege.

speaker: LAERTES
text: What part is that, my lord?

speaker: KING
text: A very ribbon in the cap of youth—

speaker: KING
text: Yet needful too, for youth no less becomes

speaker: KING
text: The light and careless livery that it wears

speaker: KING
text: Than settled age his sables and his weeds,

speaker: KING
text: Importing health and graveness. Two months since

speaker: KING
text: Here was a gentleman of Normandy.

speaker: KING
text: I have seen myself, and served against, the French,

speaker: KING
text: And they can well on horseback, but this gallant

speaker: KING
text: Had witchcraft in ’t. He grew unto his seat,

speaker: KING
text: And to such wondrous doing brought his horse

speaker: KING
text: As had he been encorpsed and demi-natured

speaker: KING
text: With the brave beast. So far he topped my thought

speaker: KING
text: That I in forgery of shapes and tricks

speaker: KING
text: Come short of what he did.

speaker: LAERTES
text: A Norman was ’t?

speaker: KING
text: A Norman.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Upon my life, Lamord.

speaker: KING
text: The very same.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I know him well. He is the brooch indeed

speaker: LAERTES
text: And gem of all the nation.

speaker: KING
text: He made confession of you

speaker: KING
text: And gave you such a masterly report

speaker: KING
text: For art and exercise in your defense,

speaker: KING
text: And for your rapier most especial,

speaker: KING
text: That he cried out ’twould be a sight indeed

speaker: KING
text: If one could match you. The ’scrimers of their nation

speaker: KING
text: He swore had neither motion, guard, nor eye,

speaker: KING
text: If you opposed them. Sir, this report of his

speaker: KING
text: Did Hamlet so envenom with his envy

speaker: KING
text: That he could nothing do but wish and beg

speaker: KING
text: Your sudden coming-o’er, to play with you.

speaker: KING
text: Now out of this—

speaker: LAERTES
text: What out of this, my lord?

speaker: KING
text: Laertes, was your father dear to you?

speaker: KING
text: Or are you like the painting of a sorrow,

speaker: KING
text: A face without a heart?

speaker: LAERTES
text: Why ask you this?

speaker: KING
text: Not that I think you did not love your father,

speaker: KING
text: But that I know love is begun by time

speaker: KING
text: And that I see, in passages of proof,

speaker: KING
text: Time qualifies the spark and fire of it.

speaker: KING
text: There lives within the very flame of love

speaker: KING
text: A kind of wick or snuff that will abate it,

speaker: KING
text: And nothing is at a like goodness still;

speaker: KING
text: For goodness, growing to a pleurisy,

speaker: KING
text: Dies in his own too-much. That we would do

speaker: KING
text: We should do when we would; for this “would” changes

speaker: KING
text: And hath abatements and delays as many

speaker: KING
text: As there are tongues, are hands, are accidents;

speaker: KING
text: And then this “should” is like a spendthrift sigh,

speaker: KING
text: That hurts by easing. But to the quick of th’ ulcer:

speaker: KING
text: Hamlet comes back; what would you undertake

speaker: KING
text: To show yourself indeed your father’s son

speaker: KING
text: More than in words?

speaker: LAERTES
text: To cut his throat i’ th’ church.

speaker: KING
text: No place indeed should murder sanctuarize;

speaker: KING
text: Revenge should have no bounds. But, good Laertes,

speaker: KING
text: Will you do this? Keep close within your chamber.

speaker: KING
text: Hamlet, returned, shall know you are come home.

speaker: KING
text: We’ll put on those shall praise your excellence

speaker: KING
text: And set a double varnish on the fame

speaker: KING
text: The Frenchman gave you; bring you, in fine, together

speaker: KING
text: And wager on your heads. He, being remiss,

speaker: KING
text: Most generous, and free from all contriving,

speaker: KING
text: Will not peruse the foils, so that with ease,

speaker: KING
text: Or with a little shuffling, you may choose

speaker: KING
text: A sword unbated, and in a pass of practice

speaker: KING
text: Requite him for your father.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I will do ’t,

speaker: LAERTES
text: And for that purpose I’ll anoint my sword.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I bought an unction of a mountebank

speaker: LAERTES
text: So mortal that, but dip a knife in it,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Where it draws blood no cataplasm so rare,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Collected from all simples that have virtue

speaker: LAERTES
text: Under the moon, can save the thing from death

speaker: LAERTES
text: That is but scratched withal. I’ll touch my point

speaker: LAERTES
text: With this contagion, that, if I gall him slightly,

speaker: LAERTES
text: It may be death.

speaker: KING
text: Let’s further think of this,

speaker: KING
text: Weigh what convenience both of time and means

speaker: KING
text: May fit us to our shape. If this should fail,

speaker: KING
text: And that our drift look through our bad performance,

speaker: KING
text: ’Twere better not assayed. Therefore this project

speaker: KING
text: Should have a back or second that might hold

speaker: KING
text: If this did blast in proof. Soft, let me see.

speaker: KING
text: We’ll make a solemn wager on your cunnings—

speaker: KING
text: I ha ’t!

speaker: KING
text: When in your motion you are hot and dry

speaker: KING
text: (As make your bouts more violent to that end)

speaker: KING
text: And that he calls for drink, I’ll have prepared him

speaker: KING
text: A chalice for the nonce, whereon but sipping,

speaker: KING
text: If he by chance escape your venomed stuck,

speaker: KING
text: Our purpose may hold there.—But stay, what noise?

speaker: QUEEN
text: One woe doth tread upon another’s heel,

speaker: QUEEN
text: So fast they follow. Your sister’s drowned, Laertes.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Drowned? O, where?

speaker: QUEEN
text: There is a willow grows askant the brook

speaker: QUEEN
text: That shows his hoar leaves in the glassy stream.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Therewith fantastic garlands did she make

speaker: QUEEN
text: Of crowflowers, nettles, daisies, and long purples,

speaker: QUEEN
text: That liberal shepherds give a grosser name,

speaker: QUEEN
text: But our cold maids do “dead men’s fingers” call them.

speaker: QUEEN
text: There on the pendant boughs her coronet weeds

speaker: QUEEN
text: Clamb’ring to hang, an envious sliver broke,

speaker: QUEEN
text: When down her weedy trophies and herself

speaker: QUEEN
text: Fell in the weeping brook. Her clothes spread wide,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And mermaid-like awhile they bore her up,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Which time she chanted snatches of old lauds,

speaker: QUEEN
text: As one incapable of her own distress

speaker: QUEEN
text: Or like a creature native and endued

speaker: QUEEN
text: Unto that element. But long it could not be

speaker: QUEEN
text: Till that her garments, heavy with their drink,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Pulled the poor wretch from her melodious lay

speaker: QUEEN
text: To muddy death.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Alas, then she is drowned.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Drowned, drowned.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Too much of water hast thou, poor Ophelia,

speaker: LAERTES
text: And therefore I forbid my tears. But yet

speaker: LAERTES
text: It is our trick; nature her custom holds,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Let shame say what it will. When these are gone,

speaker: LAERTES
text: The woman will be out.—Adieu, my lord.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I have a speech o’ fire that fain would blaze,

speaker: LAERTES
text: But that this folly drowns it.

speaker: KING
text: Let’s follow, Gertrude.

speaker: KING
text: How much I had to do to calm his rage!

speaker: KING
text: Now fear I this will give it start again.

speaker: KING
text: Therefore, let’s follow.
